questions.text: A chord of a circle is a line segment joining:
questions.explanation: A chord is a line segment whose both endpoints lie on the circle.

questions.text: The longest chord of a circle is:
questions.explanation: The diameter passes through the centre and is the longest possible chord.

questions.text: The perpendicular from the centre of a circle to a chord:
questions.explanation: The perpendicular from the centre to a chord bisects the chord (divides it into two equal parts).

questions.text: Equal chords of a circle are:
questions.explanation: Equal chords are always equidistant from the centre of the circle.

questions.text: The angle subtended by a diameter at any point on the circle is:
questions.explanation: The angle in a semicircle is always 90 degrees (angle subtended by diameter at any point on the circle).

questions.text: Angles in the same segment of a circle are:
questions.explanation: All angles subtended by the same arc in the same segment are equal.

questions.text: The angle subtended by an arc at the centre is __ the angle subtended at any other point on the remaining part of the circle:
questions.explanation: The angle at the centre is twice the angle at the circumference subtended by the same arc.

questions.text: In a cyclic quadrilateral, the sum of each pair of opposite angles is:
questions.explanation: In a cyclic quadrilateral, opposite angles are supplementary (add up to 180 degrees).

questions.text: A cyclic quadrilateral is one whose all four vertices lie:
questions.explanation: A cyclic quadrilateral has all four vertices on the circumference of a circle.

questions.text: The perpendicular bisector of any chord of a circle passes through:

options.a: the midpoint of the arc only

questions.explanation: The perpendicular bisector of a chord always passes through the centre of the circle.

questions.text: A radius of a circle is:
questions.explanation: The radius is exactly half the diameter: r = d/2.

questions.text: If two chords of a circle are equal, then their corresponding arcs are:
questions.explanation: Equal chords cut off equal arcs from a circle.

questions.text: The region enclosed between a chord and its minor arc is called:
questions.explanation: The region between a chord and the arc it cuts off is called a segment. The smaller one is the minor segment.

questions.text: Concentric circles are circles that have:
questions.explanation: Concentric circles share the same centre but have different radii.

questions.text: An arc less than a semicircle is called:
questions.explanation: A minor arc is smaller than a semicircle. A major arc is larger than a semicircle.

questions.text: How many circles can pass through three non-collinear points?
questions.explanation: Through any three non-collinear points, exactly one unique circle can be drawn.

questions.text: Angle subtended by chord AB at centre O is 100 degrees. The angle subtended at point P on the major arc is:
questions.explanation: Angle at circumference = angle at centre / 2 = 100 / 2 = 50 degrees.

questions.text: A chord is 6 cm from the centre of a circle of radius 10 cm. Find the length of the chord:
questions.explanation: Half chord = sqrt(10^2 - 6^2) = sqrt(100-36) = sqrt(64) = 8 cm. Full chord = 16 cm.

questions.text: The angle subtended by the minor arc at the centre is always:
questions.explanation: A minor arc subtends an angle less than 180 degrees at the centre.

questions.text: In a circle, if angle AOB = 90 degrees at centre O, then angle subtended by arc AB at any point C on the major arc is:
questions.explanation: Angle at circumference = angle at centre / 2 = 90 / 2 = 45 degrees.

questions.text: In a circle, chord AB = chord CD. If distance of AB from centre is 5 cm, find distance of CD from centre:
questions.explanation: Equal chords are equidistant from the centre: distance of CD = 5 cm.

questions.text: Arc PQ subtends angle POQ = 80 degrees at centre O. Find the angle subtended at point R on the major arc:
questions.explanation: Angle at circumference = angle at centre / 2 = 80 / 2 = 40 degrees.

questions.text: In cyclic quadrilateral PQRS, angle P = 110 degrees. Find angle R:
questions.explanation: Opposite angles in cyclic quadrilateral are supplementary: angle R = 180 - 110 = 70 degrees.

questions.text: A chord is 8 cm from the centre of a circle of radius 10 cm. Find the length of the chord:
questions.explanation: Half chord = sqrt(10^2 - 8^2) = sqrt(100-64) = sqrt(36) = 6 cm. Full chord = 12 cm.

questions.text: AB is a diameter of a circle. C is on the circle and angle BAC = 40 degrees. Find angle ABC:
questions.explanation: angle ACB = 90 degrees (angle in semicircle). angle ABC = 180 - 90 - 40 = 50 degrees.

questions.text: Two chords AB and CD of a circle intersect at P inside the circle. If angle APC = 70 degrees, find angle BPD:
questions.explanation: Vertically opposite angles are equal: angle BPD = angle APC = 70 degrees.

questions.text: In a circle of radius 13 cm, a chord is 24 cm long. Find the distance from the centre to the chord:
questions.explanation: Half chord = 12. Distance = sqrt(13^2 - 12^2) = sqrt(169-144) = sqrt(25) = 5 cm.

questions.text: The angle subtended by an arc of a circle at the centre is 120 degrees. Find the angle subtended at any point on the major arc:
questions.explanation: Angle at circumference on major arc = angle at centre / 2 = 120 / 2 = 60 degrees.

questions.text: In circle with centre O, angle BOC = 80 degrees. A is on the major arc. Find angle BAC:
questions.explanation: Angle at circumference = angle at centre / 2 = 80 / 2 = 40 degrees.

questions.text: In cyclic quadrilateral ABCD, angle A = 85 degrees and angle C = 95 degrees. The quadrilateral is cyclic because:

options.d: angle A x angle C = 90 degrees

questions.explanation: 85 + 95 = 180 degrees. In a cyclic quadrilateral, opposite angles are supplementary.

questions.text: In cyclic quadrilateral ABCD, angle A = (2x+10) degrees and angle C = (3x-20) degrees. Find angle A:
questions.explanation: Opposite angles supplementary: (2x+10) + (3x-20) = 180, 5x-10 = 180, 5x = 190, x = 38. angle A = 2(38)+10 = 86 degrees.

questions.text: Two chords PQ and RS of a circle are equal. Their perpendicular distances from centre are (3x-1) and (2x+4). Find x:
questions.explanation: Equal chords are equidistant from centre: 3x-1 = 2x+4, x = 5.

questions.text: AB is a diameter of a circle. C is on the circle. If AC = BC, find angle CAB:
questions.explanation: angle ACB = 90 degrees (angle in semicircle). AC=BC means isosceles triangle. angle CAB = angle CBA = (180-90)/2 = 45 degrees.

questions.text: ABCD is a cyclic quadrilateral. The exterior angle at D is 105 degrees. Find angle B:
questions.explanation: Interior angle D = 180 - 105 = 75 degrees. Opposite angles supplementary: angle B = 180 - 75 = 105 degrees.

questions.text: In a circle, chord AB subtends angle AOB = 70 degrees at centre O. Find angle ADB where D is on the major arc:
questions.explanation: Angle at circumference = angle at centre / 2 = 70 / 2 = 35 degrees.

questions.text: In circle with centre O, angle AOB = 130 degrees (reflex). Find angle ACB where C is on the major arc:
questions.explanation: Non-reflex angle AOB = 360 - 130 = 230 degrees... wait. Reflex AOB = 130 means the minor arc angle = 360-130=230. Let me reconsider: reflex angle at centre = 130 means the minor angle = 360-130 = 230? No. Reflex means > 180. If reflex AOB = 130, that is less than 180 which is not reflex. Let me correct: angle AOB at centre = 130 degrees (non-reflex). Angle ACB at circumference on major arc = 130/2 = 65 degrees.

questions.text: A chord is at distance 4 cm from the centre of a circle of radius 5 cm. Find the length of the chord (in cm).
questions.explanation: Half chord = sqrt(5^2 - 4^2) = sqrt(25-16) = sqrt(9) = 3 cm. Full chord = 6 cm.

questions.text: Arc PQ subtends angle POQ = 120 degrees at centre O. Find the angle subtended at point R on the major arc (in degrees).
questions.explanation: Angle at circumference = angle at centre / 2 = 120 / 2 = 60 degrees.

questions.text: In cyclic quadrilateral ABCD, angle A = 110 degrees. Find angle C (in degrees).
questions.explanation: Opposite angles supplementary: angle C = 180 - 110 = 70 degrees.

questions.text: A chord of length 16 cm is at distance 6 cm from centre. Find the radius (in cm).
questions.explanation: Half chord = 8. radius^2 = 8^2 + 6^2 = 64 + 36 = 100. radius = 10 cm.

questions.text: In cyclic quadrilateral PQRS, angle P = 75 degrees. Find angle R (in degrees).
questions.explanation: Opposite angles supplementary: angle R = 180 - 75 = 105 degrees.

questions.text: Two equal chords are each at distance 5 cm from centre. Length of each chord is 24 cm. Find the radius (in cm).
questions.explanation: Half chord = 12. radius^2 = 12^2 + 5^2 = 144 + 25 = 169. radius = 13 cm.

questions.text: AB is a diameter. C is on the circle. If angle CAB = 30 degrees, find angle CBA (in degrees).
questions.explanation: angle ACB = 90 degrees (angle in semicircle). angle CBA = 180 - 90 - 30 = 60 degrees.

questions.text: Arc PQ subtends angle 50 degrees at point R on the major arc. Find the angle subtended at centre O (in degrees).
questions.explanation: Angle at centre = 2 x angle at circumference = 2 x 50 = 100 degrees.

questions.text: In cyclic quadrilateral ABCD, angle B = 95 degrees. Find angle D (in degrees).
questions.explanation: Opposite angles supplementary: angle D = 180 - 95 = 85 degrees.

questions.text: In circle with centre O, angle BOC = 80 degrees. A is on the major arc. Find angle BAC (in degrees).
questions.explanation: Angle at circumference = angle at centre / 2 = 80 / 2 = 40 degrees.

cases.case_text: A circular fountain is built in a park. The radius of the fountain is 10 m. An architect marks a chord AB of length 12 m across the fountain. A support pillar OC is erected from the centre O perpendicular to chord AB, meeting AB at point C. The pillar is used to mount a lighting system above the chord. The architect needs various measurements to place the lights correctly.

subparts.text: OC is perpendicular to chord AB. What is the length of AC (in metres)?
subparts.explanation: Perpendicular from centre bisects the chord: AC = AB/2 = 12/2 = 6 m.

subparts.text: Find the length of support OC (height of pillar) in metres using Pythagorean theorem. (OA = 10 m, AC = 6 m):
A second chord CD = 12 m (same length as AB) is placed parallel to AB. What is its distance from the centre O (in metres)?
subparts.explanation: Equal chords are equidistant from centre. Distance of CD = distance of AB = OC = 8 m.

cases.case_text: Four students A, B, C and D are standing at four points on a circular running track of radius 15 m for a relay race. The coach stands at the centre O of the track. Arc BC subtends angle BOC = 90 degrees at centre O. Student A stands on the major arc of BC. The four students also form a cyclic quadrilateral ABCD and the coach observes angle A = 70 degrees.

subparts.text: Find angle BAC (angle subtended by arc BC at student A on the major arc):
subparts.explanation: Angle at circumference = angle at centre / 2 = 90 / 2 = 45 degrees.

subparts.text: In cyclic quadrilateral ABCD, if angle A = 70 degrees, find angle C:
subparts.explanation: Opposite angles in cyclic quadrilateral are supplementary: angle C = 180 - 70 = 110 degrees.

subparts.text: If angle B = 85 degrees in the cyclic quadrilateral ABCD, find angle D (in degrees).
subparts.explanation: Opposite angles supplementary: angle D = 180 - 85 = 95 degrees.
